completed.title: E1/E2 Terrain-Timescale Separation — V2 Redesign
completed.description: Ground-up redesign for MECH-058 on REE-v2 substrate. Exploits CausalGridWorld's two natural timescales: slow contamination terrain (E1 domain) vs fast env drift every 5 steps (policy domain). Three conditions: SEPARATED (e1_lr=5e-5, policy_lr=1e-3), COLLAPSED_FAST (both 1e-3), COLLAPSED_SLOW (both 5e-5). New metrics: contamination_avoidance_rate and post_drift_harm_rate directly test whether matched timescale separation yields functional benefit. Addresses V1 failure root cause: V1 GridWorld gave both LRs identical convergence terrain.

completed.title: Attribution Completion Gating — V2 Redesign
completed.description: Ground-up redesign for MECH-057 on REE-v2 substrate. Uses CausalGridWorld's info['transition_type'] == 'agent_caused_hazard' as a concrete self-attribution completion signal. Three conditions gate the REINFORCE return signal differently: COMPLETION_GATED (G = -agent_caused_harm only), FREE_REPLAN (G = -total_harm), ATTRIBUTION_BLIND (G = -env_caused_harm only). Tests whether gating the policy update to completed self-attribution events reduces agent-caused harm vs both mixed and attribution-absent baselines. Addresses V1 failure root cause: V1 GridWorld had no 'action in progress' state for gating to exploit.

completed.title: MECH-095 TPJ Agency-Detection Proxy
completed.description: Tests two conditions for TPJ comparator (MECH-095): (1) E2 motor-sensory mismatch is elevated at harm steps vs safe steps (TPJ would fire), and (2) mismatch does NOT distinguish agent_caused from env_caused harm (z_self is blind to cause type — z_world routing is necessary). completed.title: SD-003 Signed Net Eval (Benefit Signal)
completed.description: Replaces binary BCE harm_eval with signed regression net_eval trained on actual harm_signal values (normalized to [-1,1]). Tests whether E3 learning full ±0.3–0.5 value boundaries (resource=+0.3, env_hazard=-0.5, agent_hazard=-0.4) produces sharper causal_sig contrast. Root cause of plateau at 0.027: binary classifier cannot distinguish beneficial from neutral z_world. PASS: calibration_gap > 0.05.

completed.title: SD-005 Strict — Event-Conditional Separation
completed.description: Replaces EXQ-001's permissive aggregate-correlation test with event-conditional measurement. Three event types: empty_move (perspective shift only), env_caused_hazard (genuine world event), agent_caused_hazard (both). Tests C1: Δz_world(env) > Δz_world(empty) margin>0.005, C2: Δz_self(empty) > Δz_world(empty) (body selectivity — FAILED in EXQ-001 at -0.137), C3: E2 action-conditional divergence near-hazard > safe margin>0.002. Expected FAIL on C1+C2 confirming SD-005 is not functionally working.

completed.title: Perspective Shift Calibration
completed.description: Quantifies how much z_world change variance is explained by locomotion alone. Fits linear predictor z_self+action → Δz_world on empty-space steps. Computes R² on held-out empty steps (locomotion_explained_variance). Also computes perspective_shift_dominance_ratio = mean_dz_world_empty / mean_dz_world_env_hazard. PASS C1: locomotion_explained_variance > 0.3 (confirms the perspective shift problem that SD-007 must solve). Expected to PASS.

completed.title: Three-Stream Lateral Head — Harm-Salient Encoder
completed.description: Tests MECH-099 three-stream architecture. Tests C1: corrected calibration_gap > 0.05 (SD-003 threshold), C2: reafference R²_test > 0.2 (predictor learned real locomotion-induced shift), C3: Δz_corrected(empty) < Δz_raw(empty) (correction stabilised perspective).

completed.title: Combined Lateral + Reafference (MECH-099 + MECH-098)

completed.title: SD-003 Full Attribution Pipeline on CausalGridWorldV2
completed.description: The complete SD-003 test. Trains E2.world_forward on CausalGridWorldV2 transitions (separate optimizer, MECH-069). Tests whether E2 predicts action-conditional futures near hazards (C1: delta_approach > delta_none), and whether attribution distinguishes agent-caused from env-caused events (C2: attribution_gap > 0, C3: causal_sig_approach > 0.02). Depends on EXQ-029 PASS. Same world config as EXQ-029.

completed.title: SD-003 Full Attribution Pipeline v2 — Fix C1 + E3-on-E2-predictions
completed.description: Second version of EXQ-030 (3/5 FAIL). Fix 1: Drop C1 (raw delta uniform across all ttypes — dominated by 175-ch local_view, uninformative). Revised criteria: C1=attribution_gap>0, C2=causal_sig_approach>causal_sig_none, C3=causal_sig_approach>0.005 (lowered from 0.02), C4=wf_r2>0.05, C5=n_approach>=50.

completed.title: SD-003 on CausalGridWorldV2 — Seed 1 (MECH-071/ARC-024 robustness)

completed.title: SD-003 Full Attribution v2 — Seed 1 (borderline causal_sig validation)

completed.title: ARC-016 Dynamic Precision on CausalGridWorldV2 (Gradient World)

completed.title: MECH-102 Energy Escalation Under Viability Threat (E3-guided policy)

CausalGridWorldV2 with 6 hazards, 3 resources (high viability pressure). Splits steps by harm_exposure EMA: low_exposure (<0.10) vs high_exposure (>=0.20). C1: escalation_gap>0, C2: causal_sig_high>0.001, C3: n_high>=20, C4: wf_r2>0.05, C5: mean_harm_exposure_high>0.15.

completed.title: Training Depth vs. Gradient Detection Depth ('Love Expands' Computational Test)

Tests the philosophical derivation: with increasing intelligence, harm detection extends further back along the causal chain. Core criterion C4: approach_slope > contact_slope (approach detection improves faster than contact detection per episode — gradient extends backward). C1: gap_approach[1000] > gap_approach[100]+0.05. C2: gap_approach[500] > gap_approach[100]. C3: gap_approach[1000] > 0.10. C5: n_approach_min >= 20.

completed.title: ARC-025 Three-Engine Irreducibility Ablation (full vs E3-ablated vs E2-ablated)

C5: n_approach >= 50.

completed.title: MECH-069 Optimizer Separation vs. Merge Ablation

C3: cal_gap_approach_separated > 0.05. C4: wf_r2_separated > wf_r2_merged OR wf_r2_separated > 0.10. C5: n_approach >= 50.

completed.title: SD-003 Full Attribution — Large Scale (n_hazards=8, warmup=2000, eval=200)
completed.description: Root cause of EXQ-030b sign switch: n_agent_hazard_eval≈32, SE≈0.009. Seed-0 mean=+0.017 and seed-1 mean=-0.008 are both within 2 SE of zero — statistical power failure. EXQ-030c fixes: n_hazards=8 (doubles contact rate), warmup=2000 (4×), eval=200 eps × 300 steps (~200+ contact events per type). Target N: n_agent_hazard_eval >= 100. Reports mean + median + std per ttype. Core criteria same as EXQ-030b (C1=attribution_gap>0, C2=approach>none, C3=approach>0.005, C4=wf_r2>0.05). C5 changed: n_agent_hazard_eval >= 100 (primary gate for reliable sign-structure test). completed.title: MECH-102 Energy Escalation Ladder — ttype split, random policy

Fix: use RANDOM policy during eval — agent naturally enters all ttype states. Split causal_sig by transition_type (none / hazard_approach / contact=agent+env combined) instead of harm_exposure EMA. MECH-102 reformulated as escalation ladder: contact state actions are more consequential than approach, which are more consequential than none. C1: causal_sig_contact > causal_sig_none, C2: causal_sig_approach > causal_sig_none, C3: causal_sig_contact > 0.001, C4: wf_r2>0.05, C5: n_contact >= 50.

completed.title: MECH-102 Forced Escalation — E3-guided policy in dense grid (8×8, 10 hazards)

Adjusted thresholds: low_exposure < 0.05, high_exposure >= 0.10. C1: escalation_gap > 0, C2: causal_sig_high > 0.001, C3: n_high_exposure >= 50, C4: wf_r2 > 0.05, C5: mean_harm_exposure_high > 0.10.

completed.title: SD-003 Multi-Step Attribution (k=5 rollout) — fixes 1-step causal_sig dilution
completed.description: Root cause of EXQ-030b C3 FAIL: 1-step E2.world_forward produces nearly identical next-states for all actions (agent moves 1 cell/step in 12×12 grid). Causal signature is diluted to noise. Fix: k=5 multi-step rollout where only a_0 differs; shared random tail a_1..4 for actual and all counterfactuals. After 5 steps different initial actions lead to meaningfully divergent world states. C3 threshold raised from 0.005 to 0.01 to match amplified signal. Training identical to EXQ-030b (Fix 2: E3 on observed + E2-predicted). Same world as EXQ-030b.

completed.title: MECH-069 Sign Debug — Fix2 vs ObsOnly E3 training, identifies sign inversion root cause
completed.description: EXQ-035 FAIL: both separated and merged conditions have negative calibration_gap (-0.028 and -0.093). E3.harm_eval scores hazardous transitions LOWER than safe ones — backwards. EXQ-026 (obs-only E3 training) got +0.0375. Hypothesis: Fix 2 (E3 trained on E2-predicted states) causes sign inversion via distributional bias. Two sub-conditions with fresh agents (same seed): SUB-A (Fix2) = EXQ-035 setup; SUB-B (ObsOnly) = EXQ-026 setup. Also logs sigmoid-applied scores to check if it is a raw logit sign issue. PASS criteria test SUB-B: C1 cal_gap>0, C2 cal_gap>0.01, C3 approach>none ordering, C4 wf_r2>0.05, C5 n_approach>=30. Diagnostic: sign_inversion_detected flag.

completed.title: ARC-016 Precision Threshold Sweep — hazard_harm ∈ [0.005, 0.01, 0.02, 0.05, 0.10]

This experiment characterizes the quantitative relationship: does precision/commitment scale with hazard_harm? 5 fresh agents, one per hazard_harm level. Each: 300 train eps + 20 stable eval + 20 perturbed eval. Measures var_diff and commit_rate at each level. Tests MECH-093 (z_beta modulates E3 heartbeat frequency — precision boundary should scale with danger). C1: var_diff monotone increases (3+ consecutive), C2: Pearson r(hazard_harm, var_diff) > 0.6, C3: ≥3 levels with var_diff > 0.001, C4: ≥3 levels with commit_stable > commit_perturbed, C5: no fatal errors.

completed.title: Training Progression Analysis — finer checkpoints (every 50 eps) to resolve EXQ-033 C4 FAIL
completed.description: EXQ-033 FAIL: approach_slope=0.000265 ≤ contact_slope=0.000289 with only 4 checkpoints. Interpretation: the 4-checkpoint resolution is too noisy to detect slope hierarchy. EXQ-039 uses 16 checkpoints (every 50 eps over 800 total) for reliable linear regression. Uses obs-only E3 training (EXQ-026 style) to avoid Fix-2 sign inversion (see EXQ-037). C1: final_gap_approach > 0.03, C2: early monotone improvement (ep50→ep100), C3: approach_slope > 0, C4: approach_slope > contact_slope (approach calibration faster — 'love expands'), C5: n_approach_min >= 20.

completed.title: ARC-016 Precision Threshold Sweep — FIXED (E3 variance API corrected)

completed.title: SD-003 Trajectory Attribution via Hippocampal Counterfactuals

completed.title: SD-003 Trajectory Attribution — Fixed Sequential Training (E3 calibration collapse fix)
completed.description: Root cause of EXQ-043 FAIL: simultaneous terrain_prior + E3 harm_eval training shifts data distribution (approach states dominate harm_buf_pos as terrain_prior improves) causing E3 collapse to mean_harm_eval_none=0.90. Fix: three sequential phases — P1 (400 eps): terrain_prior + E2 + E1 training, E3.harm_eval frozen; P2 (200 eps): E3.harm_eval calibration with frozen terrain_prior and random policy (balanced approach/none distribution); P3 (100 eps): all frozen, attribution eval. Same trajectory attribution design as EXQ-043: causal_sig = mean(cf_scores) - selected_score over N=16 hippocampal candidates. PASS: C1-C5 same as EXQ-043 + C6 calibration gate.

completed.title: MECH-102 Energy Escalation Ladder — Ethical Policy + ttype Split
completed.description: Complements EXQ-032b (random policy PASS). Tests MECH-102 under ethical (harm-minimizing) policy: a* = argmin_{a} E3(E2(z_world, a)). With ethical policy, harm_actual = min over actions, so causal_sig inverts sign. Metric: advantage_sig = mean_cf_harm - harm_actual = how much harm did the ethical choice spare? MECH-102 prediction: advantage_sig_contact > advantage_sig_approach > advantage_sig_none — ethical agency matters most when stakes are highest. Training: identical to EXQ-032b (random policy, Fix2 E3 training). Eval: ethical policy with ttype split. If both EXQ-032b and EXQ-045 PASS, MECH-102 holds under both harm-incurred and harm-avoided framings. CAUTION: Uses Fix2 E3 training (same as EXQ-032b PASS). If EXQ-037 shows Fix2 causes sign inversion, the ethical policy (argmin E3) may be harm-seeking rather than harm-minimizing — check EXQ-037 result before interpreting. PASS: C1 adv_contact>adv_none, C2 adv_approach>adv_none, C3 adv_contact>0.001, C4 wf_r2>0.05, C5 n_contact>=50.
